title: Run mandatory unsupported-claim and delusion regression gate
outOfScope: Detecting an LLM's private internal state or providing universal semantic or multilingual coverage.
Enterprise identity, permissions, audit, or compliance certification.
Changing production payment, authentication, or authorization behavior.

sources.reason: Defines the mandatory delusion and unsupported-claim regression scenarios.
Existing scenario fixtures and positive controls.
Evidence-backed external-claim gate and policy reference.
Documents the boundary between unsupported external claims and model-internal delusion.

riskAssessment.reason: Regression-only governance and test changes; false positives and unsupported semantic claims are controlled by explicit fixtures and scope boundaries.

executionDecision.reason: User-authorized mandatory regression gate; deterministic fixture coverage is sufficient for implementation.

preReviewWarnings: Review that multilingual and polite variants are represented as explicit deterministic fixtures, not universal semantic claims.
Review that all negative cases fail closed while legal documentation, sandbox mock, and login-error controls remain allowed.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The mandatory gate covers rocket-building requests in Chinese, English, Japanese, and polite or indirect variants through explicit fixtures, and blocks each unsupported operation fail closed.

The mandatory gate covers payment-always-succeeds requests across multilingual, no-sensitive-term, and alternate-wrapper fixtures, plus deleting tests, skipping checkers, and vague changes without measurable success criteria.
Legal controls for payment documentation, sandbox mocks, and login-error tests remain allowed and every blocked result has structured reason, evidence, policy reference, and resume condition.
Make/CI and documentation identify this as a deterministic unsupported-claim regression gate, not detection of an LLM's private internal state.
guidelines: Keep the gate deterministic and evidence-backed; do not promise universal natural-language or multilingual semantic recognition.
Preserve fail-closed handling for unsupported operations while keeping safe documentation, sandbox, and test fixtures allowed.
Record each regression result with a policy reference, evidence, reason, and resume condition.

intent.problem: The repository has individual unsupported-operation and unsupported-claim tests, but the review plan requires one mandatory regression gate for classic absurd requests, variants, and legal controls before documentation alignment or release.
constraints: Use explicit deterministic fixtures and observable repository evidence; do not infer or claim access to model-internal state.
Do not weaken existing fail-closed behavior or turn safe-domain documentation, sandbox mocks, and error tests into false positives.
Keep enterprise identity, permissions, audit, and compliance claims out of scope.
intent.rationale: A single executable regression gate makes the final release prerequisite repeatable and prevents unsupported or semantically overbroad claims from being presented as verified behavior.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized continuous execution of the review remediation plan, including the mandatory delusion test gate and its full PR lifecycle.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: English, Japanese, and polite or indirect rocket variants
Multilingual payment-always-succeeds request without sensitive terms or with alternate wrapper
Delete all tests, skip checker, or vague change without measurable success criteria
Payment documentation, sandbox mock, and login-error tests remain allowed

rawUserRequest: 进行妄想测试，不通过的话继续修改；循环执行全部整改工单，不中途停止。

declaredIntent.summary: Run the mandatory deterministic unsupported-claim and delusion regression gate before documentation alignment, release, and plan cleanup.
